render_data.title: Biodegradation of three plastic samples vs time
render_data.answer: Increasing particle size: A < C < B — A biodegrades fastest (smallest particles = largest surface area), B slowest.
render_data.note: Smaller particle size gives larger surface area, so a faster rate of biodegradation.

image_prompt: SVG: line graph of biodegradation (%) versus time (days) for three plastic samples A, B and C with different particle sizes. x-axis 'Time / days' 0–50 (ticks every 10); y-axis 'Biodegradation / %' 0–60 (ticks every 10). Three saturating-growth curves all starting at the origin: A rises fastest and plateaus near 55–56%, C is intermediate plateauing near 46%, B rises slowest plateauing near 39%. Series labelled A, C, B top-to-bottom at the right ends.

render_data.title: Average time taken for the glow stick to stop glowing

yAxis.label: Time the glow stick glows / min

render_data.answer: Reading the best-fit curve at 15 °C gives approximately 330 minutes (320–340 min).
render_data.note: Scrub to 15 °C and read off the curve: ≈ 330 min. Higher temperature → faster reaction → shorter glow time.

image_prompt: SVG: scatter graph with best-fit decay curve titled 'Average time taken for the glow stick to stop glowing'. x-axis 'Temperature of water / °C' 0–70 (ticks every 10); y-axis 'Time the glow stick glows / min' 0–600 (ticks every 100). Data points approximately (5,540), (10,420), (20,260), (25,195), (30,150), (35,120), (50,70), (65,60) with a smooth exponential-decay best-fit curve through them; curve passes near 330 min at 15 °C and 250 min at 20 °C.

SVG: two-panel volcano scene replacing IB photos — left panel a black crusted lava field with bright molten orange-yellow lava rivers flowing across it; right panel a night-time volcanic crater erupting with a glowing lava fountain, spatter and smoke. Original composition and palette, copyright-safe.

render_data.title: Lewis (dot-and-cross) structure of H₂S
render_data.prompt: Select the correct Lewis (dot-and-cross) structure of H₂S, then press Check.

image_prompt: SVG: four Lewis (dot-and-cross) structure options A–D for H₂S, each H–S–H with two bonding pairs and two lone pairs on sulfur. Option A is the correct structure (two bonding pairs as a dot+cross, two lone pairs as crosses top and bottom of S); B, C, D are distractors with mis-placed electrons. Radio-button circle above each. Dots in dark blue, crosses in dark red.
Mis-crop: page-04 is question text only (Q2c period of argon, Q2d isotopes of neon) with answer boxes — no diagram. Marked text_only.

SVG: two displayed organic structures labelled A and B. A is ethene H₂C=CH₂ (two carbons joined by a double bond, two H on each carbon). B is propene CH₃–CH=CH₂ drawn as a bent skeleton (methyl-bearing carbon top-left, central CH, terminal =CH₂ on the right with the C=C double bond). Dark teal bonds and atom labels.

SVG: waste-to-energy (thermovalorization) process diagram with four numbered stages on a sky/ground scene — 1 Waste (dump truck tipping waste onto a burning pile with flames), 2 Water (a boiler/vessel of water producing steam piped onward), 3 Turbine (a bladed wheel turned by the steam), 4 Electricity (output arrow). Title 'A large machine that produces power by using hot gas to turn a wheel'.

SVG: skeletal structure of phenyl oxalate — two phenyl (benzene) rings each linked through an oxygen to a central oxalate unit –O–C(=O)–C(=O)–O–, with the ester linkage region highlighted by a soft dashed box. To the right: '+ H₂O₂' then a reaction arrow labelled 'catalyst' to 'products + light'. Caption 'Phenyl oxalate'.

SVG: five identical glass beakers of blue water, each holding a diagonal green glow stick and a red-liquid thermometer dipping in. Pink temperature-label boxes below read 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 °C. 'Diagram not to scale' top-right.

render_data.title: Glow-stick beakers — read the missing temperature

image_prompt: SVG: continuation of the glow-stick beaker apparatus — four beakers of blue water with green glow sticks and red thermometers at increasing column heights. Pink label boxes read 30, 35, 50 °C and one BLANK box (the temperature to be measured). A magnifier with 'Click' sits over the fourth thermometer.

SVG: scene of two petri dishes on a wooden bench under purple UV-light cones with a digital timer reading 0:20:00. Left dish 'UV beads exposed to UV light after 20 minutes' has beads that have changed colour (green); right dish 'Beads protected by sunscreen' has beads that stayed white. 'UV light' labels with downward arrows over each cone.

topic: Three sunscreen bottles and results table
image_prompt: SVG: three sunscreen bottles — Hi-Sun (blue, ingredients Avobenzone/Homosalate/Octisalate/Octocrylene/Oxybenzone), Solar Flex (cream-orange, Homosalate/Octisalate/Octinoxate/Titanium dioxide), Aurum (light blue, Octinoxate/Octisalate/Octocylene/Zinc Oxide). Below, a results table 'Percentage of beads that changed colour after 20 minutes in UV light' with values 100, 28, 10.

SVG: 'Energy increases' arrow above a horizontal electromagnetic-spectrum bar running IR → visible colours → UVA | UVB | UVC (dashed sub-band dividers). Below the bar, labelled emission-range arrows for 'Heat lamp' (IR/low energy), 'Light bulb' (IR through visible), 'UV lamp' (UVA region) and a dashed full-width 'Sunlight' range.
Mis-crop: page-22 is question text only (Q6a Solar Flex composition and the research question 'Is titanium dioxide the best blocker of UV light?') with an answer box — no diagram. Marked text_only.

SVG: absorbance/colorimeter apparatus on a bench — a light source emitting a beam ('Light' arrow) through a cuvette of purple sample held in a metal stand ('Sample'), into a detector ('Light meter') connected by a wavy cable to a yellow handheld meter showing '1.50' labelled 'Light absorbance'.

topic: Myporium absorbance data table and blank grid
image_prompt: SVG: results data table (Concentration of myporium / µmol dm⁻³: 0, 10, 30, 50, 70, 90, 100; Absorbance / AU: 0.00, 0.15, 0.40, 0.65, 0.60, 1.40, 1.50) above a blank plotting grid with minor and major gridlines, x-axis 'Concentration of myporium / µmol dm⁻³' 0–100 and y-axis 'Absorbance / AU' 0–1.50, for the student to plot points and a line of best fit.
